commonMistakes: Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
Treating browser APIs and language features as the same layer.
Choosing an abstraction from habit rather than required operations.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Compare primitives versus objects using a new example and justify the result.
Explain stack and heap mental model using a new example and justify the result.
Explain references using a new example and justify the result.
Explain garbage collection using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Primitives versus objects** → **Stack and heap mental model** → **References** → **Garbage collection**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Primitives versus objects.
choices.label: An execution context contains the information required to run a unit of code, including bindings, scope relationships, and a this binding. Function calls create additional contexts.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Primitives versus objects.
choices.label: Functions are first-class values: they can be stored, passed, and returned. Referring to a function is different from invoking it.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Primitives versus objects.
choices.label: A function can return a configured function that remembers creation-time context. This supports factories and reusable behavior.

choices.feedback: Primitives versus objects is best understood as follows: Assigning a primitive copies its value. Assigning an object copies a reference to the same object, so mutations are visible through every reference.
choices.label: Assigning a primitive copies its value. Assigning an object copies a reference to the same object, so mutations are visible through every reference.

questions.explanation: Primitives versus objects is best understood as follows: Assigning a primitive copies its value. Assigning an object copies a reference to the same object, so mutations are visible through every reference.

choices.feedback: Stack and heap mental model is best understood as follows: Stack versus heap is a teaching model, not a language guarantee. Use it to reason about execution lifetime and managed objects, but focus on references, reachability, and value semantics.

choices.feedback: References is best understood as follows: Object variables hold references. Equality checks identity rather than structural similarity, and shallow copies may still share nested objects.
choices.label: Object variables hold references. Equality checks identity rather than structural similarity, and shallow copies may still share nested objects.

choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.

researchSources.title: Memory Management: Garbage Collection
researchSources.usage: Coverage research only; no transcript wording is canonical.

sections.body: Assigning a primitive copies its value. Assigning an object copies a reference to the same object, so mutations are visible through every reference.
sections.title: Primitives versus objects

sections.body: Stack versus heap is a teaching model, not a language guarantee. Use it to reason about execution lifetime and managed objects, but focus on references, reachability, and value semantics.
sections.title: Stack and heap mental model

sections.body: Object variables hold references. Equality checks identity rather than structural similarity, and shallow copies may still share nested objects.
sections.title: References

sections.body: Garbage collection reclaims values that are no longer reachable. Event listeners, timers, caches, and closures can accidentally retain references and extend lifetimes.
sections.title: Garbage collection

sections.explanation: The outer object is copied but the nested reference is shared.
sections.title: Worked example

summary: Memory Management and References connects Primitives versus objects, Stack and heap mental model, References.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Memory Management and References

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns memory management and references from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **How JavaScript Works** module.
